differentials.raw: Periampullary carcinoma =  tumor arising within 1 cm from papilla of Vater

differentials.name: Malignant GIST (14%) most often beyond 1st portion of duodenum
differentials.raw: Malignant GIST (14%) most often beyond 1st portion of duodenum

context: Origin:   ampulla, pancreas, bile duct, duodenum At risk:   familial adenomatous polyposis √  may not be seen at CT / MR due to small size √  hypovascular polypoid mass at ampulla Associated with: "double duct" sign ← biliary and pancreatic ductal dilatation Prognosis:  ampullary ca. (best), pancreatic ca. (worst) √  up to 20 cm in size √  frequently ulcerated exophytic mass √  marked wall thickening √  bulky periduodenal lymphadenopathy
